question_text: Two students are trying to move a heavy box. Student A pushes with 300 N to the right, Student B pushes with 200 N to the right, and friction opposes with 450 N to the left. Explain whether the box will move or remain stationary, and justify your answer by calculating the resultant force.
correct_answer: The box will move to the right. The resultant force is 50 N to the right (300 N + 200 N - 450 N = 50 N). Since the forces are unbalanced, the box will accelerate to the right.
explanation: To solve this problem, you need to add up all the forces in the same direction, then subtract the opposing force. Forces to the right: 300 N + 200 N = 500 N. Forces to the left: 450 N. Resultant force = 500 - 450 = 50 N to the right. Since the resultant is not zero, the forces are unbalanced, so the box will accelerate and move to the right according to Newton's second law.
